candidate.name: veritas-zk/veritas
candidate.summary: Zero-knowledge proofs that an AI agent acted within its mandate without revealing the underlying data. Preventative, policy-bound verifiability for high-stakes agent actions.

digest.thesis_fit: Moderate fit (69.8/100). Matches Agent control planes / trust infra, AI x crypto / agentic, Crypto infrastructure — the agent-native, control-plane positioning the fund backs. Signal: 240 stars/day (+1,680 in 7d). Open question: Is the trust layer defensible versus incumbent wallets and the model providers?

candidate.name: mandate-labs/mandate
candidate.summary: Open policy engine that binds AI agents to user-defined permissions: spending limits, allow-lists and intent constraints enforced at settlement. Verifiable, auditable delegation for autonomous agents.

digest.thesis_fit: Moderate fit (67.9/100). Matches Agent control planes / trust infra, AI x crypto / agentic — the agent-native, control-plane positioning the fund backs. Signal: 612 stars/day (+4,300 in 7d). Open question: Is the trust layer defensible versus incumbent wallets and the model providers?

candidate.name: PayRail
candidate.summary: Neutral payment + escrow rail for agent-to-agent commerce. Agents discover services, pay and settle directly with policy-bound, atomic do-and-pay execution.

digest.thesis_fit: Moderate fit (64.1/100). Matches Agent control planes / trust infra, AI x crypto / agentic — the agent-native, control-plane positioning the fund backs. Signal: pre-seed, stealth; 3 ex-Stripe/Lightning founders. Open question: Is the trust layer defensible versus incumbent wallets and the model providers?

candidate.name: lattice-vault/signer
candidate.summary: Post-quantum signing for self-custody wallets. Drop-in ML-DSA signatures and a crypto-agility layer so chains can rotate signature schemes without a hard fork.

digest.thesis_fit: Moderate fit (50.8/100). Matches DeFi & financial infra, Post-quantum security, Consumer / social — in the fund's wider strike zone, though off the core control-plane thesis. Signal: 95 stars/day (+665 in 7d). Open question: Where does sustainable, non-incentivised demand come from?

candidate.name: Ledgerlytics
candidate.summary: Agent that automates on-chain treasury reconciliation and invoicing for crypto-native companies, with an immutable audit trail of every action it takes.

digest.thesis_fit: Watch fit (43.0/100). Matches Agent control planes / trust infra, DeFi & financial infra — the agent-native, control-plane positioning the fund backs. Signal: seed; 40% MoM revenue growth. Open question: Is the trust layer defensible versus incumbent wallets and the model providers?

candidate.name: Emberterm
candidate.summary: Unified trading terminal for prediction markets: real-time data, advanced execution and discovery across venues, plus social signal surfaces like leaderboards and wallet tracking.

digest.thesis_fit: Watch fit (40.2/100). Matches DeFi & financial infra, Consumer / social — in the fund's wider strike zone, though off the core control-plane thesis. Signal: growing fast on Farcaster; 12k MAU. Open question: Where does sustainable, non-incentivised demand come from?

candidate.name: Frameview
candidate.summary: Consumer social app for sharing on-chain portfolios as Farcaster frames. Mobile-first, no clear monetisation yet.

digest.thesis_fit: Watch fit (21.4/100). Matches Consumer / social — in the fund's wider strike zone, though off the core control-plane thesis. Signal: early; trending on CT. Open question: Is there a wedge to distribution, or does it die in a crowded field?

candidate.name: MoonRocketInu
candidate.summary: Meme coin presale promising guaranteed 1000x returns. Airdrop farming campaign live now.

digest.thesis_fit: Low fit (0.0/100). Triggers anti-signal: meme coin, anti-signal: presale, anti-signal: airdrop farming, anti-signal: 1000x — noise, not a backable pre-seed team. Screen out.

backtest.reason: Sourcing works in part (segment- or channel-specific). Narrow it to where it demonstrably works; build Memo + Pulse for the rest.

thesis:
fund: Pre-seed / early-stage fund
updated: 2026-06-23
# Crypto-native pre-seed. Durable value accrues to the control planes agents rely on.
# "Permission is the scarce resource in an age of infinite capability."
thesis_summary: >
  The fund backs agent-native crypto applications and infrastructure that abstract
  complexity, collapse workflows, execute reliably, personalise deeply, interoperate
  openly and deliver trusted outcomes. The durable winners are the control planes
  agents rely on: identity, permissions, routing, settlement, reputation and
  verifiability.

# Themes the scorer rewards. weight is relative; keywords are matched case-insensitively.
themes:
  - name: agent_control_planes
    label: Agent control planes / trust infra
    weight: 1.0
    keywords: [policy, permission, delegation, intent, guardrail, escrow, settlement,
               identity, attestation, verifiable, audit, routing, authorization, recourse]
    open_question: Is the trust layer defensible versus incumbent wallets and the model providers?
  - name: ai_x_crypto
    label: AI x crypto / agentic
    weight: 0.9
    keywords: [ai agent, autonomous, agentic, x402, agent payment, mcp, inference, llm, onchain agent]
    open_question: Does this need crypto, or is it a wrapper that a frontier lab ships natively?
  - name: defi_infra
    label: DeFi & financial infra
    weight: 0.7
    keywords: [defi, liquidity, perps, prediction market, stablecoin, vault, restaking, treasury, reconciliation]
    open_question: Where does sustainable, non-incentivised demand come from?
  - name: crypto_infra
    label: Crypto infrastructure
    weight: 0.7
    keywords: [zk, zero-knowledge, rollup, consensus, data availability, sequencer, interoperability, bridge, account abstraction]
    open_question: Is this a real bottleneck today, or premature infrastructure?
  - name: post_quantum
    label: Post-quantum security
    weight: 0.6
    keywords: [post-quantum, pqc, ml-dsa, ml-kem, lattice, quantum-safe, crypto-agility]
    open_question: Is the migration window near enough to be venture-timed?
  - name: consumer_social
    label: Consumer / social
    weight: 0.5
    keywords: [consumer, social, wallet, mobile, frames, farcaster, payments app]
    open_question: Is there a wedge to distribution, or does it die in a crowded field?

# Down-rank obvious noise.
anti_signals:
  keywords: [meme coin, presale, airdrop farming, get rich, guaranteed return, 1000x, ponzi, pump]

stage_preference:
  prefer: [pre-seed, seed, stealth, testnet, early, devnet]
  avoid: [series b, series c, public, ico, listed]

# Tunable caps (max points) for each additive signal sub-score — retune the composite
# without code changes. Values below are the built-in defaults; omit a key to keep its
# default, lower one to de-emphasise a signal, raise it to lean in. Each signal is
# reputation-weighted / ratio-based, and the cap is the most it can add on top of
# fit + traction + credibility. See docs/SIGNALS_BUILD_SPEC.md.
signal_weights:
  code_health: 15    # commit-velocity acceleration + contributor diversity (Gini)
  onchain: 12        # real stablecoin inflow + sticky non-mercenary TVL + holder spread
  team: 20           # prior exits / repeat same-domain founder / technical CEO — highest predictive value
  social: 12         # Farcaster smart-follower convergence + OpenRank, gated by Neynar score
  pre_public: 15     # retroactive grants / hackathon-with-persistence / research exit

# How the final composite blends thesis-fit against the combined quality signals.
# The score is FIT*fit + SIGNAL*signal_strength (+ credibility), so fit stays dominant
# and strong deals separate instead of all pinning at 100. Values are relative and
# normalised, so 60/40 and 0.6/0.4 are equivalent. Raise `signal` to let corroboration
# matter more; raise `fit` to lean harder on thesis match.
composite_weights:
  fit: 60       # thesis fit — the dominant component
  signal: 40    # combined quality signals (traction + code/onchain/team/social/pre_public)

thesisParsed.thesis_summary: The fund backs agent-native crypto applications and infrastructure that abstract complexity, collapse workflows, execute reliably, personalise deeply, interoperate openly and deliver trusted outcomes. The durable winners are the control planes agents rely on: identity, permissions, routing, settlement, reputation and verifiability.
